claim: The positive triangular angular coframe covers every positive determinant-one angular two-metric.
exact_scope: local real phi and shear; exact Cholesky/upper-half-plane bijection

claim: The primitive character norm is L_(p,q)=abs(p*tau-q)^2/Im(tau).
exact_scope: integral torus character lattice and positive H
remaining_open: no torus lattice is inferred on nontoric branches

claim: Every character equality locus is an explicit circle or line in the upper-half-plane chart.
remaining_open: only its no-shorter subsegment is an admissible wall

claim: Every independent co-shortest primitive pair is unimodular.
exact_scope: positive determinant-one dual metric in two dimensions
remaining_open: depends on the supplied integral lattice

claim: Every admissible wall is a GL2Z image of one reduced standard segment.
remaining_open: global monodromy can identify or exchange segments

claim: Wall interiors have two shortest unoriented lines and vertices have exactly three; three is maximal.
remaining_open: sign and physical choice are not included

claim: The shortest-character set is GL2Z covariant.
exact_scope: joint transformation of metric and character lattice

claim: A unique shortest line is locally constant along any tie-free path in a fixed lattice trivialization.
remaining_open: global sign and monodromy remain branch-dependent

claim: For a supplied character the projected connection b_w=w^T S is GL2Z covariant.
exact_scope: toric chart with the complete shift pair
remaining_open: a connection is not a phase section

claim: The shortest-character set is a global set-valued invariant wherever H and the torus lattice form a global local system.
exact_scope: toric completions and their GL2Z transitions
remaining_open: it ceases to be defined where the lattice/rank is lost

claim: A unique unoriented character sub-local-system exists on a globally tie-free branch.
exact_scope: requires tie avoidance and monodromy preservation
remaining_open: sign and phase section remain open

claim: A character sign or orientation is selected.
exact_scope: shortest lines are identified modulo sign
remaining_open: no registered premise supplies the lift

claim: A global phase section is selected.
exact_scope: projected connection may have curvature or holonomy

claim: The shear-free reciprocal diagonal swaps shortest characters through a two-way tie at phi=0.
remaining_open: no metric-only unique continuation at the tie

claim: All twelve registered finite-cell completion classes were classified exactly once without preference.
remaining_open: not a claim of all possible global four-geometries

claim: The shortest character is physically operative in UDT.
remaining_open: Reciprocity CSN finite-cell and bootstrap supply no present selector rule

claim: Total proper density selects a chamber line completion or scale.
exact_scope: no density scan or density-to-geometry law used

claim: A native carrier action source boundary completion mass or matter solution follows.
